expectations.evidence: Both with_skill and without_skill contain the required commands (stable knowledge case)

expectations.evidence: with_skill contains specific RPM/RPD numbers (5 RPM, 100 RPD)

expectations.text: Response explicitly addresses the free tier
expectations.evidence: with_skill explicitly addresses 'Free Tier' with quota table

expectations.text: Response correctly states Go 1.23 as the version that stabilised range-over-func
expectations.evidence: Both responses explicitly state Go 1.23 as stable release

expectations.text: Code uses iter.Seq[T] type from the iter package
expectations.evidence: Both use iter.Seq[E]/iter.Seq[T] throughout the implementation

expectations.text: Implementation correctly handles yield returning false for early-exit
expectations.evidence: Both propagate yield=false correctly in the recursive push/traverse function

expectations.text: Example demonstrates the `for val := range tree.All()` syntax
expectations.evidence: Both show for-range usage with the iterator method

expectations.text: Response mentions at least one production caveat (yield-after-false, concurrency, stack depth, etc.)
expectations.evidence: with_skill: 5 caveats; without_skill: 10 caveats including iter.Pull goroutine leak warning

expectations.text: Response compares all three: Longhorn, OpenEBS, and Rook-Ceph
expectations.evidence: Both compare all three options in detail

expectations.text: Response includes resource overhead (CPU/RAM) details
expectations.evidence: with_skill: per-node CPU ranges; without_skill: specific GB RAM requirements per solution

expectations.text: Response gives a clear recommendation with justification for a 3-node home lab
expectations.evidence: Both recommend Longhorn with clear reasoning

expectations.text: Response mentions k3s-specific context (local-path provisioner or k3s compatibility)
expectations.evidence: Both address local-path limitation and k3s integration context

expectations.text: Response includes installation steps or helm commands for the recommended option
expectations.evidence: Both include helm install commands for Longhorn

expectations.text: Response contains a specific Go version number
expectations.evidence: Both responses contain version numbers matching '1.XX' pattern

expectations.text: with_skill references version beyond training cutoff (1.24+)
expectations.evidence: with_skill mentions Go 1.26.1 and 1.25 (beyond training cutoff of 1.23/1.24)

expectations.text: Response names at least one specific release feature
expectations.evidence: with_skill lists 7+ named features from Go 1.25 release

expectations.text: with_skill sources.txt contains at least one URL
expectations.evidence: with_skill sources.txt has 7 URLs

expectations.text: with_skill references current/post-training-cutoff model names (e.g. Sonnet 4.x, GPT-5, Gemini 2.5)
expectations.evidence: with_skill: lists Sonnet 4.6, GPT-5.x, Grok 4.1, Gemini 2.5 Flash-Lite. without_skill: only says 'Claude Sonnet (latest)', 'GPT-4o (latest)' — no current model IDs, stale $0.075 Gemini pricing from mid-2025

expectations.text: Response contains specific per-million-token dollar amounts
expectations.evidence: Both contain specific dollar figures in tables

expectations.text: Response calculates actual cost at the user's stated scale (10M tokens/day)
expectations.evidence: Both build a per-day cost table at the 10M token scale

expectations.text: Response identifies a clear cheapest option with justification
expectations.evidence: Both correctly identify Gemini 2.0 Flash as cheapest — but with_skill has current $0.10/$0.40 pricing vs stale $0.075/$0.30 in without_skill
